questions.question: What is the main difference between 'final' and 'final class' in Java?
questions.hint: In Java, 'final' is used to prevent instance creation, whereas 'final class' is used to seal a class

questions.question: When is method overriding used in Java?
questions.hint: Method overriding is used when a subclass provides a different implementation of a method that is already defined in its superclass

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword in Java?
questions.hint: The 'super' keyword in Java is used to access the members of the superclass or to invoke the superclass's constructor
